2605.21146.title: Detecting Trojaned DNNs via Spectral Regression Analysis
2605.21146.paper_summary: Modern DNNs are repeatedly fine-tuned to incorporate new data and functionality. This evolutionary workflow introduces a security risk when updated data cannot be fully trusted, as adversaries may implant Trojans during fine-tuning. We present MIST, a Trojan detection approach that analyzes how a model's internal representations change during fine-tuning. Rather than attempting to reconstruct trigger conditions, MIST characterizes benign model evolution using pre-activation spectra and flags updates whose spectral deviations are inconsistent with this reference. This framing treats Trojan detection as a regression problem over model updates. An empirical evaluation across four datasets and eight Trojan attacks shows that spectral distances reliably distinguish Trojaned updates from clean fine-tuning. MIST outperforms state-of-the-art detection accuracy after a single update, without requiring any knowledge about the poisoned data or the trigger, and remains effective under multi-step benign evolution, with graceful and bounded degradation. These results indicate that spectral evolution provides a stable and assumption-light signal for detecting malicious model updates.
2605.21146.paper_summary_zh: 現代的深度神經網絡（DNN）會不斷進行微調，以納入新的數據和功能。當更新後的數據無法完全信任時，這種演變工作流程會引入安全風險，因為對手可能在微調過程中植入木馬。我們提出了MIST，一種木馬檢測方法，分析模型在微調過程中內部表示的變化。MIST並不試圖重建觸發條件，而是利用預激活光譜來描述良性模型的演變，並標記那些其光譜偏差與此參考不一致的更新。這種框架將木馬檢測視為模型更新的回歸問題。對四個數據集和八個木馬攻擊的實證評估顯示，光譜距離可靠地區分了木馬更新和乾淨的微調。MIST在單次更新後超越了最先進的檢測準確性，無需任何有關被污染數據或觸發器的知識，並在多步良性演變下保持有效，且退化平穩且有界限。這些結果表明，光譜演變提供了一個穩定且假設輕量的信號，用於檢測惡意模型更新。
2605.21146.author: Samuele Pasini et.al.
2605.21146.authors: Samuele Pasini, Jinhan Kim, Paolo Tonella
